page: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile with clear tradeoffs. The instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. However, multi-core scaling and memory performance present notable bottlenecks. Multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU physics tests fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is a primary limitation, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10% and large-block read/write bandwidths categorized as weak.

Given its hardware profile, the c6a.xlarge offers a qualitatively cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or high multi-threaded throughput. It is suitable for single-threaded processing, basic web serving, and lightweight text processing. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded software compilation, or complex mathematical simulations due to its constrained memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, the instance exhibits weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth, with PassMark memory scores falling in the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the c6a.xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for single-threaded workloads, lightweight text processing, and basic web serving, while rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive database operations or highly parallelized computing tasks.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, strong single-core performance, and a balanced entry-level memory configuration.

8.0 GB of DDR4 system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
